primary_detection.name: URL Encoded Data Exfiltration
primary_detection.description: Detected URL encoding used to hide directory traversal and sensitive file access paths

underlying_threats.name: Directory Traversal
underlying_threats.description: Uses ../ sequences to access files outside intended directory

underlying_threats.name: Sensitive File Exfiltration
underlying_threats.description: Reads and transmits /etc/passwd, /etc/shadow, and SSH keys to external server

underlying_threats.name: System Credential Theft
underlying_threats.description: Targets password hashes and SSH keys for credential compromise

reasoning: URL encoding used to conceal directory traversal attacks and sensitive file paths. The decoded payload accesses system credential files and SSH keys, then exfiltrates them to an external server.
